remaining_main_risk: The three credibility checks in §VIII.D are non-gold robustness analyses. (A) Evidence Necessity uses a pre-existing NLI audit on the same SimClaim backbone but an earlier dataset version; the TF-IDF sanity check is a surface-level probe. (B) Screening Utility counts depend on silver labels and may shift under gold. (C) Label-Shift is a simulated sensitivity probe — the relabeled strong_action samples are not gold data, and at 50% retention the Δ 95% CI crosses zero ([−0.026, +0.255]), so the 50% result is positive-but-statistically-weaker and must not be reported as a robust pass. Independent gold adjudication (§VII) remains the only mechanism that can validate absolute numbers, resolve the mild/strong boundary κ, and confirm the strong_action retention rate. Until gold is completed, all §VIII.D conclusions are directional silver-stage support, not validation.
recommended_next_action: Send V3.12 to advisor as the current best silver-stage draft. Frame §VIII.D as: 'three non-gold credibility checks that strengthen the silver-stage relative pattern (R4 > LLM on strong_action) without replacing gold adjudication.' Highlight the label-shift break-even (~50% retention) as a pre-registered quantitative bar for the gold pilot: ≥70% robust, 60% marginal-positive, 50% CI-crosses-zero, <50% downgrade per §VII.G. Proceed to 50-sample gold pilot per §VII protocol, prioritizing the high_risk_sample_bank cases. Do NOT claim gold validation in any external communication.

auxiliary.new_section: §VIII.D Robustness and Credibility Checks Before Gold Adjudication
auxiliary.renamed_section: §VIII.D What Remains Unresolved -> §VIII.E What Remains Unresolved
auxiliary.abstract_sentence_added: We further report three non-gold credibility checks (§VIII.D) testing evidence necessity, screening utility, and sensitivity to simulated strong-label shift.

at_50pct.strength: positive but statistically weaker / CI crosses zero
